message:
feat(wizard): add contextual help button with per-step troubleshooting (bd-1yfv)

Add a "Need help?" button to the wizard layout that opens a native
<dialog> with step-specific troubleshooting content, tips, and a
one-click debug info export for support requests.

- Create lib/stepHelp.ts with help content for 9 steps (common
  issues as collapsible details, tips, and getDebugInfo utility)
- Create components/wizard/HelpPanel.tsx using native <dialog> for
  built-in focus trap and Escape handling
- Integrate HelpPanel into both desktop and mobile wizard headers

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1pjj","title":"Deep exploration: GIIL (Get Image from Internet Link)","description":"## Goal
Perform deep exploration of GIIL (GitHub Image Inline Links) and revise its description on the flywheel/TLDR pages with comprehensive testing.

## Phase 0: Pre-flight Verification (CRITICAL)

### 0.1 Tool Existence Check
```bash
# Verify GIIL installation
[[ -d /dp/giil ]] && echo "PASS: giil repo exists" || { echo "FAIL: giil repo missing"; exit 1; }
command -v giil &>/dev/null && echo "PASS: giil command available" || { echo "FAIL: giil not in PATH"; exit 1; }

# Check for image processing tools
command -v convert &>/dev/null && echo "INFO: ImageMagick available" || echo "INFO: ImageMagick not installed"
```

### 0.2 Content Snapshot (BEFORE State)
```bash
SNAPSHOT_DIR=/tmp/giil-exploration-snapshots-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)
mkdir -p $SNAPSHOT_DIR
cp apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before
cp apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before
echo "Snapshots saved to $SNAPSHOT_DIR"
```

### 0.3 TypeScript Interface Reference
Content must match FlywheelTool interface:
- id, name, tagline, description, deepDescription
- features[], cliCommands[], connectsTo[]
- integrationLevel, category, status

## Phase 1: Research (GATHER CONTEXT)

### 1.1 Primary Documentation
- `cat /dp/giil/README.md` - Read full README
- Check for supported cloud services (iCloud, Dropbox, Google Photos)

### 1.2 Code Investigation
- Launch code investigation agent to understand:
  - URL parsing for cloud services
  - Full-resolution image extraction
  - Authentication handling (if any)
  - Output format options
  - Batch processing capabilities

### 1.3 CLI Command Verification
```bash
# Verify each documented command works
giil --help 2>&1 | head -20
giil download --help 2>&1 | head -10

# Test with a sample URL (dry run if available)
giil --dry-run "https://example.com/image" 2>&1 | head -10 || echo "No dry-run"
```

### 1.4 External Context Search
- `/xf search 'giil OR image download OR icloud photos'` - Twitter archive
- `cass search 'giil image extract' --robot --limit 10` - Past sessions

### 1.5 Project State Review
- Check beads in /dp/giil/.beads/
- Review recent commits: `cd /dp/giil && git log --oneline -20`

## Phase 2: Analysis (SYNTHESIZE UNDERSTANDING)

### 2.1 Core Capabilities Verification
Document findings for each area:
- [ ] Supported cloud services (iCloud, Dropbox, Google Photos)
- [ ] URL parsing mechanism
- [ ] Full-resolution extraction method
- [ ] Output format options
- [ ] Batch processing

### 2.2 Synergy Verification
Cross-reference these tools actually integrate:
- [ ] mail - image attachment handling
- [ ] wa - web automation for extraction

### 2.3 Service Verification
```bash
# Check supported services in code
grep -r "icloud\|dropbox\|google" /dp/giil/src/ 2>/dev/null | head -10

# Check URL patterns supported
grep -r "pattern\|regex\|url" /dp/giil/src/ 2>/dev/null | head -10
```

## Phase 3: Revision (UPDATE DESCRIPTIONS)

### 3.1 Update apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts
Update giil entry with VERIFIED information:
- `tagline`: Full-resolution image extraction
- `description`: Cloud service image download
- `deepDescription`: How extraction works per service
- `features`: Verified supported services
- `cliCommands`: Only commands that actually work
- `connectsTo`: Only verified integrations

### 3.2 Update apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts
Update TldrFlywheelTool entry with:
- `briefDescription`: Technical summary
- `bulletPoints`: Verified capabilities
- `synergyExamples`: Working integration examples

## Phase 4: Testing (VERIFY CHANGES)

### 4.1 TypeScript Compilation
```bash
cd apps/web && npx tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | head -20
```

### 4.2 Unit Tests
```bash
# Test giil entry structure
node -e "
const { flywheelTools } = require('./lib/flywheel');
const giil = flywheelTools.find(t => t.id === 'giil');
console.log('Testing giil entry...');
console.assert(giil, 'giil entry exists');
console.assert(giil.features?.length > 0, 'has features');
console.assert(giil.cliCommands?.length > 0, 'has commands');
console.log('All assertions passed');
"
```

### 4.3 E2E Test: Image Extraction (Safe Mode)
```bash
#\!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
LOG=/tmp/giil-e2e-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S).log

echo "=== GIIL E2E Test ===" | tee $LOG
echo "Started: $(date)" | tee -a $LOG

# Test 1: Help
echo "Test 1: Help..." | tee -a $LOG
giil --help 2>&1 | head -10 | tee -a $LOG

# Test 2: Version/status
echo "Test 2: Version..." | tee -a $LOG
giil --version 2>&1 | tee -a $LOG || echo "No version flag"

# Test 3: Supported services
echo "Test 3: Services..." | tee -a $LOG
giil services 2>&1 | head -5 | tee -a $LOG || echo "No services command"

echo "=== All Tests Passed ===" | tee -a $LOG
echo "Log: $LOG"
```

### 4.4 Content Diff Verification
```bash
echo "=== Changes Made ==="
diff $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts || true
diff $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts || true
```

## Phase 5: Completion (FINALIZE)

### 5.1 Rollback Procedure (if tests fail)
```bash
cp $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts
cp $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts
echo "Rolled back to pre-exploration state"
```

### 5.2 Sync Changes
```bash
br update bd-1pjj --status done
br sync --flush-only
```

### 5.3 Final Verification
- [ ] All tests pass
- [ ] TypeScript compiles without errors
- [ ] Supported services documented correctly
- [ ] No broken links or references","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-26T20:03:01.750430866Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T21:37:58.610182628Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

-{"id":"bd-1yfv","title":"Help button for stuck users in web wizard","description":"## Overview
Add a contextual "I'm stuck" help button in the web wizard that provides step-specific troubleshooting guidance.

## Current Problem
- Users get stuck and don't know what to do
- Generic FAQs don't address specific step issues
- No easy way to get help without leaving the wizard
- Common mistakes aren't addressed proactively

## Proposed Solution
- Floating "Need help?" button on each step
- Opens modal with step-specific troubleshooting
- Common issues and solutions for that step
- Links to relevant documentation
- Option to copy debug info for support

## Help Content Structure
```typescript
interface StepHelp {
  commonIssues: {
    symptom: string;
    solution: string;
    link?: string;
  }[];
  tips: string[];
  debugInfo: () => string;
}
```

## Example: SSH Step Help
**Common Issues:**
- "Permission denied" → Check file permissions (chmod 600)
- "Connection refused" → Verify VPS is running, check port 22
- "Host key verification" → Add host to known_hosts

**Tips:**
- Test connection before proceeding
- Use key-based auth, not passwords

## Implementation Details
1. Create `HelpButton` component (floating, fixed position)
2. Create `HelpModal` with tabbed content
3. Define help content for each wizard step
4. Track help button usage for analytics

## Debug Info Export
```
# ACFS Debug Info
Step: ssh-setup
OS: macOS 14.2
Browser: Chrome 120
State: { provider: "hetzner", sshKeyPath: "~/.ssh/id_rsa" }
Errors: []
```

## Test Plan
- [ ] Test help button appears on all steps
- [ ] Test modal opens/closes correctly
- [ ] Test content matches current step
- [ ] Test debug info generation
- [ ] Test mobile layout (button position)

## Files to Create
- apps/web/components/wizard/HelpButton.tsx
- apps/web/components/wizard/HelpModal.tsx
- apps/web/lib/stepHelp.ts (help content per step)","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:02:21.414394609Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T22:39:49.487879012Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1yfv","depends_on_id":"bd-3y1n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:04:26.493434121Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1zkz","title":"Smoke test issue - DELETE ME","status":"closed","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T16:23:14.588348366Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T16:23:35.774459690Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T16:23:35.773402068Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-21kh","title":"Progress bar during tool installation","description":"## Overview
Add a visual progress bar during tool installation showing overall progress and current tool being installed.

## Current Problem
- Installation can take several minutes
- Users see scrolling text but no sense of overall progress
- No way to estimate time remaining
- Anxiety: "Is it stuck or still working?"

## Proposed UI
```
Installing ACFS tools...
[████████████░░░░░░░░] 12/26 tools (46%)
Current: Installing rust toolchain...
```

## Implementation Details
1. Count total tools to install upfront
2. Update progress after each tool completes
3. Show current tool name during install
4. Use ANSI escape codes for in-place updates
5. Respect NO_COLOR environment variable

## Progress Calculation
- Parse manifest.yaml for tool count
- Subtract already-installed tools
- Increment counter after each install_tool() call

## Terminal Handling
```bash
# In-place update (same line)
printf '\r[%-20s] %d/%d tools (%d%%)' "$bar" "$done" "$total" "$pct"

# With NO_COLOR or non-TTY
echo "[$done/$total] Installing $tool..."
```

## Graceful Degradation
- Non-interactive: Simple line-by-line output
- NO_COLOR: Text-only progress
- Narrow terminal: Abbreviated format

## Test Plan
- [ ] Test progress updates correctly
- [ ] Test NO_COLOR fallback
- [ ] Test non-TTY output (piped)
- [ ] Test narrow terminal handling
- [ ] Visual test: progress matches actual completion

## Files to Modify
- scripts/lib/install_helpers.sh (add progress tracking)
- scripts/install.sh (initialize progress bar)
- New: scripts/lib/progress.sh (progress bar utilities)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:01:45.195454182Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T23:07:22.822272754Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-21kh","depends_on_id":"bd-3y1n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:03:54.194943376Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-31ps.2","title":"Add acfs support-bundle command (diagnostics pack)","description":"## Background
Support/debugging requires scattered manual steps (logs, state.json, doctor output, versions). A single support bundle simplifies triage and reduces back-and-forth.

## Goal
Add an `acfs support-bundle` command that collects diagnostics into a timestamped archive with redaction.

## Scope
- New script in scripts/lib/support.sh.
- New CLI entrypoint via acfs command dispatcher.
- Bundle includes: state.json, install logs, doctor --json, versions, OS info, selected config files, recent errors.

## Considerations
- Redact tokens/secrets (API keys, auth tokens).
- Size limits and opt-in for large logs.
- Output should be safe to share.

## Testing
- Unit tests for redaction + file collection.
- Docker E2E: create bundle and verify contents.

## Acceptance Criteria
- One command produces a shareable bundle with manifest + logs + doctor JSON.
- Redaction verified and documented.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:22:17.754579012Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T23:22:17.754579012Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.2","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:22:17.754579012Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-31ps.2.1","title":"Support-bundle implementation + CLI wiring","description":"## Goal
Implement the support-bundle collection logic and CLI entrypoint.

## Tasks
- Create scripts/lib/support.sh with functions: collect_files, capture_doctor_json, capture_versions, capture_env_summary.
- Add `acfs support-bundle` command routing (acfs wrapper / doctor.sh dispatch table).
- Output bundle directory: ~/.acfs/support/<timestamp>/ plus .tar.gz archive.
- Include a manifest JSON describing included files and versions.

## Considerations
- Ensure stdout stays clean for piping; write progress to stderr via logging.sh.
- Avoid requiring root; use sudo only when needed for logs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Running `acfs support-bundle` produces a tarball + manifest JSON.
- Bundle includes doctor JSON and state.json when present.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:22:37.265721256Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T22:40:31.376687322Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T22:40:31.376618843Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:22:37.265721256Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:41:52.853108258Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:42:22.564866490Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

## Tasks

- Implement redaction in support.sh (regex-based, with allowlist for safe values).
- Provide a summary report of redactions performed.
- Add a `--no-redact` flag for advanced users (with warning).

- Ensure false positives are minimal (e.g., Git SHAs not redacted).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Bundle safe to share by default.
- Redaction summary included in manifest JSON.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:22:55.389196201Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T23:22:55.389196201Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.2.2","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:22:55.389196201Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

## Tasks

- Log output to /tmp/acfs_support_bundle_test_TIMESTAMP.log with pass/fail summary.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests fail if required bundle components are missing.
- E2E run produces detailed logs + summary.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:23:05.075169407Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T23:42:51.658165458Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:23:05.075169407Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:42:39.178930848Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-31ps.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-31ps.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:42:51.657404163Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-31ps.3","title":"Structured install logging + JSON summary artifacts","description":"## Background
Install failures are hard to diagnose without durable logs. We want deterministic artifacts: a log file and a JSON summary of phases, durations, and failures.

## Goal
Add structured install logging (log file + JSON summary) without polluting stdout.

## Scope
- Default log file under ~/.acfs/logs/install_YYYYmmdd_HHMMSS.log.
- JSON summary artifact with phase timings, failed step, and resume hints.
- Redaction for sensitive values in logs.

## Considerations
- Respect --quiet and --dry-run behaviors.
- Preserve stderr progress output.

## Testing
- Unit tests for summary JSON and redaction.
- Integration test in tests/vm to assert artifacts exist.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Every install run produces a log file and summary JSON.

+/**
+ * Per-step help content for the "Need help?" wizard button.
+ *
+ * Each step optionally defines common issues (symptom + solution)
+ * and general tips. Steps without entries show a generic fallback.
+ *
+ * @see bd-1yfv for the full spec
+ */
+
+import { getUserOS, getVPSIP } from "./userPreferences";
+import { getCompletedSteps, TOTAL_STEPS } from "./wizardSteps";
+
+export interface StepIssue {
+  symptom: string;
+  solution: string;
+}
+
+export interface StepHelp {
+  commonIssues: StepIssue[];
+  tips: string[];
+}
+
+export const STEP_HELP: Partial<Record<number, StepHelp>> = {
+  1: {
+    commonIssues: [
+      {
+        symptom: "I don't know which OS to pick",
+        solution:
+          "Choose the operating system on the computer you'll be typing commands on. If you're on a Mac laptop, pick Mac. If you're on a Windows desktop, pick Windows.",
+      },
+    ],
+    tips: [
+      "This just affects which terminal instructions you see — you can change it later.",
+      "Linux users skip the terminal installation step automatically.",
+    ],
+  },
+
+  2: {
+    commonIssues: [
+      {
+        symptom: "I already have a terminal, do I need another one?",
+        solution:
+          "If you're on Mac, the built-in Terminal.app works fine. On Windows, we recommend Windows Terminal from the Microsoft Store for a better experience.",
+      },
+      {
+        symptom: "Windows Terminal won't install from the Store",
+        solution:
+          "Try downloading it directly from GitHub: search 'Windows Terminal GitHub releases' and grab the latest .msixbundle file.",
+      },
+    ],
+    tips: [
+      "Any terminal that supports SSH will work — the suggested ones just have the best experience.",
+    ],
+  },
+
+  3: {
+    commonIssues: [
+      {
+        symptom: "ssh-keygen: command not found",
+        solution:
+          "On Windows, make sure you're using PowerShell or Windows Terminal (not Command Prompt). On Mac/Linux, open a new terminal window.",
+      },
+      {
+        symptom: "The key file already exists",
+        solution:
+          "If you already have an SSH key at ~/.ssh/acfs_ed25519, you can skip this step and reuse it. Just make sure you remember the passphrase (if any).",
+      },
+      {
+        symptom: "Permission denied when creating the key",
+        solution:
+          "Make sure the ~/.ssh directory exists: run 'mkdir -p ~/.ssh && chmod 700 ~/.ssh' then try again.",
+      },
+    ],
+    tips: [
+      "Ed25519 keys are shorter and more secure than RSA keys.",
+      "The passphrase is optional but recommended for security.",
+    ],
+  },
+
+  5: {
+    commonIssues: [
+      {
+        symptom: "Which VPS plan should I pick?",
+        solution:
+          "For the agent coding flywheel, pick the cheapest plan with at least 4GB RAM and Ubuntu 24.04. Hetzner's CX22 ($4.50/mo) is the best value.",
+      },
+      {
+        symptom: "I entered my IP but can't continue",
+        solution:
+          "Make sure you're entering the IPv4 address (looks like 123.45.67.89), not a hostname. Check your VPS provider's dashboard for the IP.",
+      },
+      {
+        symptom: "My VPS shows 'initializing' or 'building'",
+        solution:
+          "Wait 1-2 minutes for the VPS to finish provisioning. Refresh your provider's dashboard to check the status.",
+      },
+    ],
+    tips: [
+      "Copy the IP address directly from your provider's dashboard to avoid typos.",
+      "Make sure you selected Ubuntu 24.04 (not 22.04 or Debian) when creating the VPS.",
+    ],
+  },
+
+  6: {
+    commonIssues: [
+      {
+        symptom: "Connection refused",
+        solution:
+          "The VPS might still be starting up. Wait 30 seconds and try again. Also verify the IP address is correct.",
+      },
+      {
+        symptom: "Connection timed out",
+        solution:
+          "Check that your IP address is correct. If using a firewall or VPN, try disabling it temporarily. Your provider may need a few minutes to provision the server.",
+      },
+      {
+        symptom: "Permission denied (publickey)",
+        solution:
+          "This usually means the SSH key isn't being found. Make sure you're using: ssh -i ~/.ssh/acfs_ed25519 root@YOUR_IP",
+      },
+      {
+        symptom: "Host key verification failed",
+        solution:
+          "If you recreated your VPS, the old key is cached. Run: ssh-keygen -R YOUR_IP  then try connecting again.",
+      },
+    ],
+    tips: [
+      "The first connection will ask you to verify the host fingerprint — type 'yes' to continue.",
+      "If using Windows, make sure you're in PowerShell, not Command Prompt.",
+    ],
+  },
+
+  8: {
+    commonIssues: [
+      {
+        symptom: "'bash' is not recognized (Windows)",
+        solution:
+          "You're running commands on your local machine instead of the VPS. Make sure you SSH into your VPS first, then run the preflight commands.",
+      },
+      {
+        symptom: "APT is locked by another process",
+        solution:
+          "Another update is running. Wait a few minutes, or run: sudo kill $(cat /var/lib/dpkg/lock-frontend 2>/dev/null) then try again.",
+      },
+      {
+        symptom: "Cannot reach github.com",
+        solution:
+          "Check your VPS network: try 'ping 8.8.8.8'. If that works but github.com doesn't, try 'echo nameserver 8.8.8.8 | sudo tee /etc/resolv.conf'.",
+      },
+    ],
+    tips: [
+      "Run all preflight commands on the VPS (your SSH session), not your local machine.",
+    ],
+  },
+
+  9: {
+    commonIssues: [
+      {
+        symptom: "Installation appears stuck or frozen",
+        solution:
+          "Tool compilation (especially Rust tools) can take 5-15 minutes. The installer shows progress — watch for the spinner. Don't close the terminal.",
+      },
+      {
+        symptom: "SSH connection dropped during install",
+        solution:
+          "Reconnect via SSH and re-run the installer. It's safe to run multiple times — it skips already-installed components.",
+      },
+      {
+        symptom: "Permission denied errors during install",
+        solution:
+          "Make sure you're running as root (your prompt should show root@). If you connected as ubuntu, run: sudo -i  then re-run the installer.",
+      },
+    ],
+    tips: [
+      "The installer is safe to run multiple times — it won't break anything.",
+      "Keep your SSH session open until you see the completion message.",
+    ],
+  },
+
+  10: {
+    commonIssues: [
+      {
+        symptom: "I can't connect as ubuntu",
+        solution:
+          "Use: ssh -i ~/.ssh/acfs_ed25519 ubuntu@YOUR_IP. If that fails with 'permission denied', connect as root and check that the installer completed successfully.",
+      },
+    ],
+    tips: [
+      "After this step, always connect as 'ubuntu' (not 'root') for daily work.",
+    ],
+  },
+
+  12: {
+    commonIssues: [
+      {
+        symptom: "acfs doctor shows failures",
+        solution:
+          "Re-run the installer: it's safe to run multiple times. Make sure you're connected as the ubuntu user, then run: source ~/.zshrc && acfs doctor",
+      },
+      {
+        symptom: "Command not found: acfs",
+        solution:
+          "Run: source ~/.zshrc  — the acfs command is loaded from your shell configuration. If that doesn't work, the installer may not have completed.",
+      },
+    ],
+    tips: [
+      "Run 'source ~/.zshrc' if commands aren't found after installation.",
+      "The status check verifies all installed tools — failures here mean the installer needs to be re-run.",
+    ],
+  },
+};
+
+/**
+ * Build a debug info string for the current wizard state.
+ * Useful for users to share when seeking support.
+ */
+export function getDebugInfo(currentStep: number): string {
+  const os = getUserOS();
+  const ip = getVPSIP();
+  const completed = getCompletedSteps();
+  const ua =
+    typeof navigator !== "undefined" ? navigator.userAgent : "unknown";
+
+  return [
+    "# ACFS Wizard Debug Info",
+    `Step: ${currentStep} of ${TOTAL_STEPS}`,
+    `OS: ${os ?? "not selected"}`,
+    `VPS IP: ${ip ?? "not entered"}`,
+    `Completed steps: ${completed.length > 0 ? completed.join(", ") : "none"}`,
+    `Browser: ${ua}`,
+    `Time: ${new Date().toISOString()}`,
+  ].join("\n");
+}
